title: Cannot run podman info getting cannot clone: Operation not permitted
body:
### Issue Description

Hey, I'm trying to run podman info inside my container but it does not work because I get cannot clone: Operation not permitted, thats my dockerfile

```
FROM ubuntu:22.04

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
RUN echo "APT::Get::Assume-Yes \"true\";" > /etc/apt/apt.conf.d/90assumeyes

RUN apt-get update \
&& apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        ca-certificates \
        curl \
        jq \
        git \
        iputils-ping \
        libcurl4 \
        libicu-dev \
        libunwind8 \
        netcat \
        libssl3 \
        wget \
        apt-transport-https \
        software-properties-common \    
        podman \
		zip \
		unzip \
&& rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

RUN podman info \
  && alias docker=podman 

# Install Node.js
RUN curl -sL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_18.x -o nodesource_setup.sh \
    && bash nodesource_setup.sh \
    && apt-get install -y nodejs \
    && rm nodesource_setup.sh \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install .Net 
RUN wget -q https://packages.microsoft.com/config/ubuntu/22.04/packages-microsoft-prod.deb -O packages-microsoft-prod.deb \
  && dpkg -i packages-microsoft-prod.deb \
  && apt-get update \
  && apt-get install -y dotnet-sdk-8.0 \
  && rm packages-microsoft-prod.deb \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Kiota
RUN wget -q https://github.com/microsoft/kiota/releases/download/v1.13.0/linux-x64.zip -O kiota.zip \
  && unzip -q kiota.zip -d /usr/local/bin/ \
  && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/kiota \
  && rm kiota.zip

RUN curl -LsS https://aka.ms/InstallAzureCLIDeb | bash \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /azp

COPY ./start.sh .
RUN chmod +x start.sh

CMD ["./start.sh"]
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Use my dockerfile


### Describe the results you received

cannot clone: Operation not permitted

### Describe the results you expected

Version of the installed podman

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

comments.body: how was the outer container created?  Is it a podman container or any other kind?
It is docker container created with docker build, and while building I get this error @giuseppe
the default seccomp profile in Docker blocks `clone(CLONE_NEWUSER)`.  You need to use a different seccomp profile, or disable seccomp

What exactly is seccomp? I will use podman in this container only for building and pushing images, not for running so do I need that? Also how do I disable
It? @giuseppe

yes, "building" an image still requires running a container.

You can disable it with `--security-opt seccomp=unconfined`

I use this flag and I still get this error @giuseppe 
```
docker build --security-opt seccomp=unconfined -t registry.gitlab.com/varico/docker-registry/azure-devops-pipeline-agent:latest -f Dockerfile .
```
```
=> ERROR [ 4/11] RUN podman info   && alias docker=podman                                                                                                                                                           0.5s 
------
 > [ 4/11] RUN podman info   && alias docker=podman:
0.415 time="2024-05-06T15:01:33Z" level=warning msg="\"/\" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers"
0.415 cannot clone: Operation not permitted
0.415 Error: cannot re-exec process
```
does the error happen if you use podman for the outer container?
I've not tested with podman, only with docker, you think that should change something? @giuseppe
Using podman it seems it built the image

Later when my azure agent have a deploy job where he builds a couple of images it gives again this error
Cannot clone: Operation not permitted
```
time="2024-05-06T17:44:18Z" level=warning msg="\"/\" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers"
cannot clone: Operation not permitted
time="2024-05-06T17:44:18Z" level=error msg="invalid internal status, try resetting the pause process with \"podman system migrate\": cannot re-exec process"
Warning: run-commands command "podman build -t registry.gitlab.com/varico/docker-registry/s30-api-gateway:20240506.11 -f apps/api/gateway/varico-s30-gateway-api/Dockerfile . && podman push registry.gitlab.com/varico/docker-registry/s30-api-gateway:20240506.11" exited with non-zero status code
```
@giuseppe

if it works with Podman, and it doesn't with Docker, then it is clearly a problem with Docker.

You must be able to run something like `unshare -r true` inside the Docker container.

Are you able to run that command in the Docker container?  It looks like `--security-opt seccomp=unconfined ` had no effect

The first problem I had was when I tried to use podman info inside dockerfile in RUN, and you've told me to try to build container with podman instead of docker, and that worked, but right now I get error when I try build container image using podman inside container, so I don't use docker anymore, only podman @giuseppe 
If something is unclear please tell me

If you will take a look at the last line of output here you can see that I try build container using podman inside container, is it even possible to use podman inside container? @giuseppe 

https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22606#issuecomment-2096591794

I've tried using buildah and I get error like this
```
time="2024-05-06T23:14:03Z" level=error msg="error parsing PID \"\": strconv.Atoi: parsing \"\": invalid syntax"
buildah build --security-opt label=disable --security-opt seccomp=unconfined -t registry.gitlab.com/varico/docker-registry/s30-api-gateway:20240506.3 -f apps/api/gateway/varico-s30-gateway-api/Dockerfile .
```

I've tried locally your Dockerfile and it works well with Podman 5 on Linux 6.8.  I'd first suggest to use a newer Podman.  The version present in Ubuntu 22.04 is 3.4.4, that is very old.


Can you run a command like `unshare -U echo hello` on your host?
I use ubuntu 22.04, I can't find available repository with podman with version 5.x @giuseppe

Also as I mentioned, Dockerfile works for me too, but later when my container gets request to build a container inside it it fails like this, is something wrong with permissions thats why I get Operation not permitted? Do I need to setup something else to make it work or what, because for right now I just download podman inside my container then I try to use it
https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22606#issuecomment-2096591794
Please run `unshare -U echo hello` both on the host and inside `docker build` and tell me what you get
I get hello in response on host and container, also i use podman build if that changes something @giuseppe

Okey I probably found issue, when I run my azure agent container where I build my containers with podman on portainer it gives me all of those Operation not permitted errors, but when I tried to run same container and do same things on my windows it does not give any errors and it works as it should. Any idea why on portainer it does not work? @giuseppe
I am not familiar with that environment but it seems to be blocking the creating of a new user namespace.  In this restricted environment you can't use Podman
